sources.title: 2020 Census DHC P1 Total Population by ZCTA
sources.coverage: 2020; 33,774 ZCTAs in the DHC response
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: 2020 fixed population denominator

sources.title: 2020 Gazetteer: ZIP Code Tabulation Areas
sources.coverage: 2020 ZCTAs for the 50 states, DC, and Puerto Rico
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: ZCTA representative points and land area

sources.title: 2020 ZCTA to County Relationship File
sources.coverage: 2020 national ZCTA-to-county land intersections
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: Assign ZCTA to the state containing its largest land-area share

sources.title: 2020 Census Apportionment Results
sources.coverage: Resident population by state and decade through the 2020 Census
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: Independent 2020 CONUS-plus-DC resident-population coverage denominator

sources.title: Spatially interpolated PM2.5 concentrations for the US for 2021
sources.coverage: CONUS, daily 2021, 15 km grid
sources.license: No explicit reuse license located in repository metadata; cite the dataset
sources.role: Independent-model validation example using the released 2021 median-background grid; not an input to the Stanford annual series

sources.title: Spatially interpolated non-smoke and smoke PM2.5 concentrations for the US: 2024 mean-background grid
sources.coverage: CONUS, daily 2024, 15 km grid; standalone file in the 2006-2024 Dryad v5 release
sources.role: Pinned independent-model 2024 processor target using the publisher-recommended mean background. No 2024 output is checked into this release because automated retrieval was blocked; it is never appended to Stanford as the same estimator

sources.title: Spatially interpolated non-smoke and smoke PM2.5 concentrations for the US: 2006-2023 mean-background grids
sources.coverage: CONUS, daily 2006-2023, 15 km grid; 18 annual netCDF members in one 5.71 GB archive
sources.role: Pinned optional all-year independent-model input. The streaming processor supports it, but the release workstation intentionally does not download this multi-GB archive when storage is constrained

sources.title: Wildfire Smoke PM2.5 Predictions v2.0 beta
sources.organization: Stanford Environmental Change and Human Outcomes Lab
sources.coverage: U.S. counties, daily 2006-2023
sources.license: Preliminary beta data; no explicit reuse license stated on the Stanford distribution page
sources.role: Controlling 2006-2023 annual website series and validation reference

sources.title: Annual Resident Population Estimates for Counties, 2010 to 2020
sources.coverage: July 1, 2020 county estimates for the United States
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: Fixed July 1, 2020 county population weights for Stanford aggregation

sources.title: Daily local-level estimates of ambient wildfire smoke PM2.5 for the contiguous US
sources.coverage: U.S. counties, daily 2006-2020 (version 1, not the website's v2 beta)
sources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0
sources.role: Archived prior-version reference; not used to calculate the 2006-2023 dashboard series

sources.title: AQS Daily Data, PM2.5 parameters 88101 and 88502
sources.coverage: CONUS and DC regulatory monitor-day observations, 2006-2026; 2026 lags the operational AirNow supplement
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: Official total-PM2.5 source for the consistent 2006-2026 AQS/HMS screen. Event-included summaries are retained; daily bulk files do not expose specific RF/RT/RM fire qualifier codes

sources.title: AQS Qualifier Codes
sources.coverage: Current and legacy AQS qualifier definitions, including RF Canadian fire, RT U.S. wildfire, and RM prescribed fire
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: Defines fire qualifier semantics. The codes are not present in the bulk daily summaries and are therefore documented but not imputed into the monitor screen

sources.title: AirNow Daily Data File: PM2.5 24-hour site averages
sources.coverage: U.S. daily PM2.5-24hr monitoring sites, 2026-06-01 through 2026-07-18; preliminary operational supplement
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: Preliminary daily PM2.5 supplement after the lagged AQS 2026 snapshot; combined with HMS only in the explicitly non-comparable operational proxy

sources.title: Hazard Mapping System Smoke Polygons
sources.coverage: Daily satellite-analyst smoke plume polygons, 2005-present; annual bundles processed for 2006-2026
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: Smoke-presence screen for every 2006-2026 monitor-day; includes U.S. and Canadian smoke reaching the U.S. but does not identify plume origin

sources.title: Integrated Surface Database Global Hourly present-weather observations
sources.organization: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information
sources.coverage: Global hourly station observations, 1901-present; North American coverage increases sharply in the 1940s and 1970s
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: Candidate pre-2006 observation-context index using explicit smoke present-weather codes; not PM2.5 exposure and not comparable across changing station coverage

sources.title: MTBS Fire Occurrence Points
sources.organization: USGS EROS and USDA Forest Service
sources.coverage: Large U.S. fires, 1984-2026 current archive; 2025-2026 incomplete/provisional
sources.license: No restrictions on use except reasonable and proper source acknowledgement
sources.role: Source-covered normalized U.S. large-wildfire records, annual counts and acres, and non-attributive tooltip context; not smoke exposure

sources.title: Spatial wildfire occurrence data for the United States, 1992-2020 (FPA FOD sixth edition)
sources.organization: USDA Forest Service Research Data Archive
sources.coverage: United States, 2.3 million deduplicated final-fire reports, 1992-2020
sources.license: U.S. government-funded data; usable without additional permissions or fees with citation
sources.role: Best available comprehensive U.S. all-size wildfire occurrence backbone; complements rather than replaces MTBS mapped large-fire area

sources.title: InFORM Fire Occurrence Data Records
sources.coverage: United States public wildfire occurrence records, 2021-2026 snapshot; 565,764 retrieved records include approved and unapproved records and ongoing historical incorporation
sources.grain: one public fire occurrence record; certification and final-report status retained
sources.license: No explicit licence field located on the ArcGIS item; preserve NIFC attribution and source disclaimer
sources.role: Dated post-2020 U.S. incident catalog lane. Live-service changes during retrieval are recorded in its receipt; counts are not official annual statistics and are not appended to FPA as a trend

sources.title: AQI Breakpoints
sources.coverage: Current PM2.5 AQI breakpoints; table updated July 14, 2026
sources.role: Defines 35.5 micrograms per cubic meter as the lower PM2.5 concentration breakpoint for AQI 101 and supplies the official category scale

sources.title: National Burned Area Composite annual summary
sources.organization: Natural Resources Canada, Canadian Forest Service
sources.coverage: Canada, complete annual adjusted burned area, 1972-2025; revision 20260513
sources.grain: one Canada-wide adjusted burned-area total per fire year
sources.license: Open Government Licence - Canada
sources.role: Canadian burned-area context; fire counts intentionally omitted because NBAC does not supply a homogeneous count series

sources.title: Canadian National Fire Database large-fire point records
sources.organization: Natural Resources Canada, Canadian Forest Service
sources.coverage: Canadian reported large-fire point records, 1946-2025 in this release; completeness varies by agency and year
sources.license: Open Government Licence - Canada
sources.role: Source-covered normalized Canadian large-fire records, annual rollup and non-attributive tooltip context; never causal attribution for U.S. smoke exposure

sources.title: Canadian National Fire Database all reported fire point records
sources.organization: Natural Resources Canada, Canadian Forest Service
sources.coverage: Canada, provincial and territorial reported fire records 1950-2025 in the current archive; agency-year completeness varies and early coverage is partial
sources.license: Open Government Licence - Canada, subject to source-agency terms recorded in NFDB metadata
sources.role: Best available comprehensive Canadian reported-fire occurrence backbone; agency-year coverage flags are required before trend analysis

sources.title: Incident Management Situation Report
sources.organization: National Interagency Fire Center / National Interagency Coordination Center
sources.coverage: United States, 2026 year-to-date through 2026-07-18
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: Current 2026 U.S. fire count and acres-burned context; preliminary and not spliced into MTBS history

sources.title: Reported Fire Statistics by Response Type
sources.coverage: Canada, 2026 year-to-date through 2026-07-18
sources.license: Open Government Licence - Canada
sources.role: Current 2026 Canadian fire count and area-burned context; preliminary and not spliced into NBAC history

sources.title: Canadian Wildland Fire Information System
sources.coverage: Canadian active fires, fire weather, and smoke products
sources.role: Canadian fire context and future attribution validation

sources.title: We charted every wildfire since 1984. There's been a drastic increase
sources.coverage: Published July 2026; MTBS-based historical fire context
sources.role: Inspiration and journalistic context; MTBS controls reproduced values

sources.title: The Great Smoke Pall - September 24-30, 1950
sources.coverage: Documented September 1950 transboundary smoke episode
sources.role: Pre-instrumental historical episode context only

sources.title: Smoke from Canadian Fires Blankets Eastern U.S.
sources.coverage: July 2002 Quebec wildfire-smoke episode
sources.role: Pre-2006 documented U.S. air-quality episode context only

sources.title: What's with all the smoke the last few years?
sources.coverage: Published July 18, 2026
sources.role: Event/context lead; underlying Canadian fire and temperature datasets must control any reused values

sources.title: Thick wildfire smoke lingers over Mid-Atlantic early Saturday
sources.coverage: Published July 18, 2026
sources.role: Documented 2026 episode and lead to operational NOAA/EPA products

sources.title: CWFIS active fires and estimated smoke for July 18
sources.coverage: Published July 18, 2026
sources.role: Event-attribution lead for Ontario/northwestern Canadian fires; CWFIS controls measurements

sources.title: Air Pollution and Cigarette Equivalence
sources.coverage: Published 2015; rough PM2.5 health-impact analogy
sources.role: Hover-card context only; supplies the approximate 22 micrograms per cubic meter for one day per cigarette-equivalent rule

sources.title: 2025 World Air Quality Report
sources.coverage: Calendar 2025 city-level annual PM2.5; released March 24, 2026
sources.role: Hover-card context only; supplies Loni, India annual average PM2.5 of 112.5 micrograms per cubic meter

sources.title: Air Quality Life Index Methodology
sources.organization: Energy Policy Institute at the University of Chicago
sources.coverage: Long-term PM2.5 exposure and life-expectancy methodology
sources.role: Hover-card context only; supplies the 0.98 life-years per sustained additional 10 micrograms per cubic meter relationship

sources.title: Documented U.S. wildfire-smoke events, 1950-2005
sources.coverage: 19 accepted documentary U.S. wildfire-smoke episodes from 1950 through 2005; intentionally non-exhaustive
sources.grain: one interpreted and reviewed smoke episode
sources.license: Project-authored factual compilation under repository MIT license; original sources retain their own terms
sources.role: Controlling pre-2006 documentary event table; never a complete incidence census or comparable PM2.5 series

sources.title: Observation evidence for documented U.S. wildfire-smoke events
sources.coverage: 27 government-source observation records supporting the accepted 1950-2005 event catalog
sources.grain: one source-specific observation or evidentiary statement
sources.license: Project-authored factual compilation under repository MIT license; original sources retain their own terms
sources.role: Traceable evidence behind each interpreted historical event

sources.title: Source citations for documented U.S. wildfire-smoke events
sources.coverage: 27 authoritative government or published source records used by the historical evidence catalog
sources.license: Project-authored factual citation index under repository MIT license; original sources retain their own terms
sources.role: Original-source URLs and authority metadata for the documentary event and observation tables

sources.title: NOAA Storm Events annual detail files
sources.organization: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information
sources.coverage: Annual detail files 1950-2005; Wildfire is present as an event type only from 1996 in these files
sources.license: U.S. federal government data; public domain
sources.role: Deterministic wildfire-incident candidate and narrative smoke-keyword screen; changing event types and narrative practices prevent a complete smoke series

research_papers.title: Daily Local-Level Estimates of Ambient Wildfire Smoke PM2.5 for the Contiguous US
research_papers.citation: Childs, M. L., et al. Environmental Science & Technology 56, 13607-13621 (2022).
research_papers.role: Published method for the Stanford v1 daily 10 km and local-area wildfire-smoke PM2.5 estimates.

research_papers.title: Growing wildfire-derived PM2.5 across the contiguous U.S. and implications for air quality regulation
research_papers.citation: Childs, M. L., et al. EarthArXiv preprint, revised August 18, 2025.
research_papers.role: Method paper identified by Stanford for the v2.0 beta dataset that controls this report's 2006-2023 estimates.

research_papers.title: Contribution of Wildland-Fire Smoke to US PM2.5 and Its Influence on Recent Trends
research_papers.citation: O'Dell, K., Ford, B., Fischer, E. V. & Pierce, J. R. Environmental Science & Technology 53, 1797-1804 (2019).
research_papers.role: Published monitor-plus-satellite interpolation method underlying the independent O'Dell/Ford smoke grids.

research_papers.title: The contribution of wildfire to PM2.5 trends in the USA
research_papers.role: National 2000-2022 research context for wildfire smoke's effect on U.S. PM2.5 trends.

research_papers.title: Summertime Trends in U.S. Population Exposure to Fine Particulate Air Pollution (PM2.5) from Wildfire Smoke
research_papers.citation: Volckens, J. Environmental Science & Technology Letters 11, 1182-1186 (2024).
research_papers.role: Independent 2000-2023 monitor and satellite-plume analysis of population exposure and high-PM2.5 smoke events.

research_papers.title: Long-range PM2.5 pollution and health impacts from the 2023 Canadian wildfires
research_papers.role: Transboundary attribution and U.S. population-exposure context for Canadian wildfire smoke in 2023.

research_papers.title: Health and Regulatory Impacts of PM2.5 From Wildland Fires for 2019-2024 in the US
research_papers.citation: Jaffe, D. A., et al. GeoHealth 10 (2026).
research_papers.role: Latest published national monitor and satellite-smoke analysis extending research context through 2024.

research_papers.title: The Great Smoke Pall - September 24-30, 1950
research_papers.role: Primary published documentation for the 1950 transboundary episode; contextual, not comparable PM2.5 exposure data.

context_artifacts.role: Curated trace of supplied 2026 article and social-post event leads
Values preserved from the supplied USA Today annual MTBS graphic; official MTBS controls the pipeline
Values preserved from the supplied USA Today seasonal graphic; official MTBS controls the pipeline
